serviceDescription.aws: AWS IoT Core is a managed cloud service that lets connected devices easily and securely interact with cloud applications and other devices.
serviceDescription.aws-cn: Amazon IoT Core is a managed cloud service that lets connected devices easily and securely interact with cloud applications and other devices.
serviceDescription.aws-us-gov: AWS IoT Core is a managed cloud service that lets connected devices easily and securely interact with cloud applications and other devices.

components.label: Number of devices (MQTT)
components.description: Enter the number of devices you will utilize.

components.label: Connection duration
components.description: Choose a connection duration or enter the number of connection minutes (Custom)

components.label: Average number of connection minutes for a device

description.aws: Enter the number of minutes a device will be connected to AWS IoT Core in a month.
description.aws-cn: Enter the number of minutes a device will be connected to Amazon IoT Core in a month.

subComponents.variableLabel: Average number of connection minutes for a device

operands.variableLabel: Average number of connection minutes for a device

text.aws: To estimate your monthly costs, enter the number and average size of messages you expect to transmit.  You are charged for messages sent in both directions, from a device to AWS IoT Core and from AWS IoT Core to a device.  Per message pricing is the same for MQTT, HTTP, and WebSockets.
text.aws-cn: To estimate your monthly costs, enter the number and average size of messages you expect to transmit.  You are charged for messages sent in both directions, from a device to Amazon IoT Core and from Amazon IoT Core to a device.  Per message pricing is the same for MQTT, HTTP, and WebSockets.

components.label: Number of messages for a device

description.aws: Enter the number of messages transmitted between a device and AWS IoT Core in a month.
description.aws-cn: Enter the number of messages transmitted between a device and Amazon IoT Core in a month.

components.label: Average size of each message
components.description: Messages are metered in 5 KB increments; an 8 KB message is metered as two messages.

operands.variableLabel: Number of messages for a device

components.label: Number of Shadow operations for a device
components.description: Enter the number of operations that access or modify the Device Shadow in a month.

components.label: Average size of each record
components.description: Operations are metered in 1 KB increments; an update to a 1.5 KB Device Shadow record is metered as two operations.

operands.variableLabel: Number of Shadow operations for a device

components.label: Number of Registry requests
components.description: Enter the number of Registry requests in a month.

components.label: Average size of each record
components.description: Requests are metered in 1 KB increments; a request that returns a 1.5 KB Registry record is metered as two requests.

components.label: Number of rules triggered
components.description: Enter the number of rules triggered per month by a device.

components.label: Average number of actions executed per rule
components.description: Enter the average number of actions executed per rule by a device.

components.label: Average size of each message
components.description: Rules and actions are metered in 5 KB increments; a rule with two actions that is executed for a 7 KB message is metered as two rules and four actions.

operands.variableLabel: Average number of actions executed per rule

templates.title: Telemetry using Basic Ingest
templates.description: This pricing calculator option helps you price a telemetry workload in which a number of devices send a constant number of messages monthly.

helpText.aws: Basic Ingest enables you to securely send device data to the AWS services supported by AWS IoT rule actions without incurring messaging costs. Basic Ingest optimizes data flow by removing the publish/subscribe message broker from the ingestion path, so it's more cost effective.
helpText.aws-cn: Basic Ingest enables you to securely send device data to the Amazon supported by Amazon IoT rule actions without incurring messaging costs. Basic Ingest optimizes data flow by removing the publish/subscribe message broker from the ingestion path, so it's more cost effective.
helpText.aws-us-gov: Basic Ingest enables you to securely send device data to the AWS services supported by AWS IoT rule actions without incurring messaging costs. Basic Ingest optimizes data flow by removing the publish/subscribe message broker from the ingestion path, so it's more cost effective.

text.aws: You do not incur an AWS IoT Core - Messages cost for messages sent or received using the reserved topics of Basic Ingest. Messages sent to AWS IoT Core via Basic Ingest are processed by the AWS IoT Core - Rules Engine directly. The Rules Engine will then filter, transform and route the messages to other AWS services (Kinesis, S3, …) using actions.  Additionally, the Rules Engine can store message data in an AWS IoT Core – Shadow. Data stored in Shadow can then be queried using AWS IoT Device Management - Fleet Indexing.
text.aws-cn: You do not incur an Amazon IoT Core - Messages cost for messages sent or received using the reserved topics of Basic Ingest. Messages sent to Amazon IoT Core via Basic Ingest are processed by the Amazon IoT Core - Rules Engine directly. The Rules Engine will then filter, transform and route the messages to other Amazon services (Kinesis, S3, …) using actions.  Additionally, the Rules Engine can store message data in an Amazon IoT Core – Shadow. Data stored in Shadow can then be queried using Amazon IoT Device Management - Fleet Indexing.
